# services.varnish

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.varnish`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.varnish.config` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Verbatim default.vcl configuration. |
| `services.varnish.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Varnish Server. |
| `services.varnish.enableConfigCheck` | `boolean` | Whether to enable checking the config during build time. |
| `services.varnish.extraCommandLine` | `string` | Command line switches for varnishd (run 'varnishd -?' to get list of options) |
| `services.varnish.extraModules` | `list of package` | Varnish modules (except 'std'). |
| `services.varnish.http_address` | `null or string` | HTTP listen address and port. |
| `services.varnish.listen` | `list of (submodule)` | Accept for client requests on the specified listen addresses. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.address` | `string` | If given an IP address, it can be a host name ("localhost"), an IPv4 dotted-quad ("127.0.0.1") or an IPv6 address enclosed in square brackets ("[::1]"). (VCL4.1 and higher) If given an absolute Path ("/path/to/listen.sock") or "@" followed by the name of an abstract socket ("@myvarnishd") accept connections on a Unix domain socket. The user, group and mode sub-arguments may be used to specify the permissions of the socket file. These sub-arguments do not apply to abstract sockets. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.group` | `null or string` | Group name who owns the socket file. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.mode` | `null or string` | Permission of the socket file (3-digit octal value). |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.name` | `null or string` | Name is referenced in logs. If name is not specified, 'a0', 'a1', etc. is used. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.port` | `null or 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | The port to use for IP sockets. If port is not specified, port 80 (http) is used. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.proto` | `one of "HTTP", "PROXY"` | PROTO can be 'HTTP' (the default) or 'PROXY'. Both version 1 and 2 of the proxy protocol can be used. |
| `services.varnish.listen.*.user` | `null or string` | User name who owns the socket file. |
| `services.varnish.package` | `package` | The varnish package to use. |
